data.title: Market ceiling may be too low; plan sized for wrong market
data.narrative: The model caps the market base at €360M, cut down from €495M using a 10–12% growth rate. But that growth rate comes from a €8.5–9.5B report covering a far broader category than rack PDUs, so the fix imported the same error it claimed to remove. Germany alone is forecast at $509.6M by 2031, already above the whole base. If the true base sits at €400M or more, the Year-1 plan is sized for the wrong market.

mechanism.text: The growth rate used to cut the ceiling came from a broad multi-category report, not rack PDUs.
Germany alone reaches $509.6M by 2031 and global anchors span $1.7B to $3.94B.
A low base sizes the Year-1 plan too small for certification and channel needs.

data.whyMissable: Cutting an inflated ceiling reads as rigor, so nobody audits the number that justified the cut.
data.falsifier: A dedicated rack-PDU report showing the EU base at or below €360M for 2025/2026.
data.likelihoodRationale: Several broader datapoints point above the band, but each carries the same category-breadth uncertainty. The true rack-PDU base remains untested behind paywalled reports, so the ceiling is unproven rather than proven wrong.
